data.change_summary: The current tree already includes the requested producer metadata fix: workflow composition now derives `RecordProducer::Agent` from `InvocationEnvelope.metadata.backend_used` and `InvocationEnvelope.metadata.model_used`, and the prompt-review, completion, final-review, and single-stage persistence paths use that metadata-backed producer when writing payload/artifact history records. No additional `src/adapters/fs.rs` struct edit was required because `ArtifactRecord` already persists the populated `producer` field via serde serialization.
outstanding_risks: `AgentExecutionService` still normalizes `backend_used` and `model_used` to the resolved target before orchestration reads the envelope, so there is not yet an end-to-end test proving divergent adapter-reported metadata flows into persisted artifacts.

steps.description: Inspect the current workflow artifact persistence paths and confirm whether producer metadata is sourced from invocation metadata.
Apply any missing metadata-backed `RecordProducer` persistence changes in workflow composition and artifact record handling.
Run targeted Rust validation for the producer helper, final review flow, and compile health.

validation_evidence: `nix develop -c cargo check --quiet` passed; it emitted pre-existing warnings in `src/contexts/conformance_spec/runner.rs` about the unused `spawned` variable/assignments.

Code inspection confirms `src/contexts/workflow_composition/mod.rs` builds `RecordProducer::Agent` from `InvocationMetadata.backend_used.family` and `InvocationMetadata.model_used.model_id`, and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs` persists artifacts using that helper-derived producer.

Code inspection confirms `ArtifactRecord` already carries `producer: Option<RecordProducer>` in `src/contexts/project_run_record/model.rs`, and `src/adapters/fs.rs` writes artifact JSON via `serde_json::to_string_pretty(artifact)`, so the populated producer fields are serialized without further adapter changes.
